text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check individual parts of the code in isolation, ensuring each function works correctly. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different components work together smoothly. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps them think carefully about what the code should do from the start. Ultimately, thorough testing builds confidence. When a project has good test coverage, developers can make changes or add new features without worrying about breaking existing functionality. This saves time and reduces stress in the long run.
